academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a secondary school diploma suitable for higher education, must satisfy country-based entry-title rules, and must compete through the CISIA CEnT-S ranking procedure.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Upper secondary school diploma (or equivalent) with at least 12 years of schooling, to be obtained by 31 July 2026.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: No fixed school-subject prerequisites are stated; ranking is based on the CEnT-S Mathematics and Reasoning on texts and data sections.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Country-based specific entry title requirements apply. The CEnT-S normalised weighted score must be at least 10, and the upper secondary diploma must be presented by 31 July 2026.

accepted_proofs: A recognised English certificate meeting the University minimum scores, such as TOEFL iBT 80, IELTS 6.0, Cambridge B2 First 173, or PTE Academic 65.
A previous qualification completed entirely in English, documented through self-certification or an official statement showing the language of instruction.
A provisional statement of results from the certifying body is accepted while waiting for the final certificate.
exemption_rules: Applicants whose entry qualification was completed entirely in English are exempt from submitting a separate English certificate.
Native English speakers from recognised countries, including the United States, are exempt from submitting a separate English certificate.

application_deadline_eu.notes: The official apply page labels the May-June window as 'Call Two for EU and EU-equated students'. The ranking list for the first EU round is published from 7 May 2026.

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The official apply page also states that 30 places are reserved for non-European candidates residing outside Italy.

required_documents.notes: Required for English-taught bachelor's applications and explicitly listed in the programme calls.
The EU call accepts a valid identity document or passport; the non-EU abroad call requires a passport.

required_documents.document_name: Proof of registration, provisional results, and normalised results of the CEnT-S test
required_documents.notes: Upload the provisional results after the test and the normalised results at the end of the macro-period.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records and upper secondary school diploma
required_documents.notes: The diploma must reflect at least 12 years of schooling; if it has not yet been awarded, it may be provided later by 31 July 2026.

required_documents.document_name: Official translations into Italian or English
required_documents.notes: Required if the educational documents are issued in another language.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption.

required_documents.document_name: Certificate of entrance examination to access university
required_documents.notes: Required if the law of the country where the upper secondary certificate was awarded makes such an examination mandatory for university access.
Required only for non-EU citizens residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: Legalisation, apostille, or CIMEA statement of verification for the upper secondary diploma
required_documents.notes: Successful applicants must submit one of these by 31 October 2026 to complete enrolment.

entry_exam_or_test.dates: First macro-period: 27 November 2025, 16 December 2025, 15 January 2026 (resit 20 January 2026). Second macro-period: 19 February 2026, 26 February 2026, 12 March 2026 (resit 19 March 2026).
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The ranking uses Mathematics and Reasoning on texts and data. The normalised weighted score must be at least 10. Students with fewer than 5 correct answers in Mathematics receive an OFA in Mathematics.

source_quotes.quote: This is a limited access programme
score higher than or equal to 10
first semester will be entirely held online
Call for non-EU students: 7 January 2026 – 7 March 2026

uncertainty_notes: Some official University pages formerly referred to this programme as Philosophy, Politics and Economics (PPE), but the active 2026/27 apply page and the current admission call PDFs use Economics, Governance and Decision-Making.
The official calls state that activation of the first year remains subject to accreditation under Ministerial Decree 1154/2021.
